metadata.note: Shared charged-sector budget artifact. The current constructive candidate is the monoidal completion of the common family spectral seed sigma_seed = mean(family_eigenvalues) + min(spectral_gaps) on the canonical decomposable charged direct-sum family; the remaining base theorem burden is the decomposition-independent gluing theorem showing that the u/d/e certificates are restrictions of one common evaluator.

notes: This artifact isolates the odd quark shape-map burden from the quark response builder. The current constructive candidate is the shared-seed pullback Hilbert-Schmidt form on the explicit odd quotient basis, with no independent odd scalar slot and no per-block lift constants.
The odd codomain has no independent odd scalar slot on the current theorem design, so delta_logg_q=0 is a quotient corollary of the odd-form closure rather than a separate local quark theorem.

metadata.note: Blind forward dictionary artifact for flavor-theorem development.

notes: This artifact now tracks a projective polar-Riesz common-refinement eigenline transport candidate. Same-label diagonal transport is explicit here; the off-diagonal flavor-edge overlaps are induced downstream after transport and are not the transport maps themselves.
The remaining OPH-native blocker is the common-refinement eigenline transport functor itself, not the downstream projector-overlap algebra on the current family.

metadata.note: Overlap-edge transport cocycle candidate is now treated as an induced off-diagonal edge object downstream of the projective same-label eigenline transport candidate, not as a primitive transport law. The remaining OPH-native blocker is the eigenline transport functor behind the transported projectors.

Explicit odd-response-law boundary for the quark lane. The current constructive candidate is the single-kappa signed projector ray on the shape-only odd codomain, with the dual covector taken from the shared-seed pullback Hilbert-Schmidt form. The common charged norm remains a separate shared-law question.

Candidate odd quark-sector descent built from an explicit shared charged response seed plus the separate odd-response-law artifact. The local shape map is now explicit and coefficient-free on the current single-kappa ray; the remaining theorem burdens are the upstream OPH origin of that ray and the shared charged scalarization closure for absolute norms.
